articles.title: Would Drake's Flood of New Music Be Stronger as One Great 13-Song Album?
articles.why: GQ's exercise — distilling Drake's three-album drop into a single 13-song classic — is the sharpest critical take on the trilogy yet, and the most useful framing for anyone trying to understand what actually worked and why the volume undercuts the quality.
articles.hook: Three Drake albums dropped at once / GQ cut it to 13 songs / The edit is better than the release.

articles.title: "Make Them Pay" Producer Ovrkast. Talks Drake's Relationship With Bay Area Rap
articles.why: The producer behind one of Iceman's standout tracks explaining how he built it on a Mac Dre sample is the real cultural story inside Drake's Bay Area pivot — it maps a lineage between hyphy and whatever Drake is doing now, and that lineage actually matters.
articles.hook: Drake sampled Mac Dre on Iceman / The producer who did it just explained why / Bay Area rap never really left.

articles.title: ‘Star Wars: Mandalorian & Grogu’ Hopes To Hyper-Space To $160M Global Opening Over Memorial Day Weekend – Box Office Preview
articles.why: The first Star Wars theatrical release in seven years opens Memorial Day weekend with a $160M projected global opening — a high-stakes test of whether Disney can revive its most valuable franchise after years of streaming-first decisions.
articles.hook: Star Wars returns to theaters this weekend / First time in seven years / Disney needs this badly.

articles.title: Molly Santana Says 'Everything About' Nicki Minaj is 'F*cking Special'
articles.why: Molly Santana's profile matters because she's the underground figure Drake chose to elevate on Iceman — understanding who she is and why Drake picked her is the missing context for anyone following the album's cultural footprint.
articles.hook: Drake put an underground rapper on Iceman / Nobody knew her name before this week / Now they do.
